chunk_summary: This section outlines the workflow for catch-up immunization referrals during follow-up for moderate wasting, emphasizing feeding, counselling, and home diet therapy as part of nutritional recovery and immunization adherence.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of catch-up immunization during moderate wasting follow-up.
Learn the referral workflow for immunization in malnourished children under 5 years.
Identify dietary and counselling strategies to support recovery from moderate acute malnutrition (MAM).
Integrate immunization guidelines with nutritional therapy for holistic care.
key_facts: Moderate wasting (MAM) requires both nutritional therapy and immunization adherence.
Catch-up immunization is guided by the National Immunization Schedule (India).
Mission Indradhanush targets immunization gaps in children under 5 years.
IMNCI guidelines provide standard treatment protocols for malnourished children aged 2 months to 5 years.
Nutritional oedema and wasting management are critical for reducing mortality in children under 5 years.
reference_quote: Mission Indradhanush aims to ensure full immunization coverage for children under 2 years and pregnant women, addressing gaps in routine immunization services. (Source: Mission Indradhanush Guidelines)
document_key_topics: Integration of nutritional and immunization guidelines
